images.caption: Figure 2:Latent Trajectories of Standard vs. Elastic Looped Transformers.Xkint{X_{k}}^{\text{int}}&Xkmax{X_{k}}^{\text{max}}represent output of intermediate (LintL_{\text{int}}) & final loops (LmaxL_{\text{max}}) respectively forkt​hk^{th}generation sampling step. Unlike standard recurrent models (left) where only the final iterationXkmax{X_{k}}^{\text{max}}reaches the solution space, our ILSD training (right) guides intermediate statesXkint{X_{k}}^{\text{int}}also toward the target space. This transformation shifts the model from a fixed-depth architecture to an Any-Time inference framework, supporting flexible computational budgets through early exits within a sampling step.

Figure 3:Overview of ELT framework. Training (left) utilizes shared parametersΘ\Thetawith recurrent loops and Intra-Loop Self Distillation (ILSD) to improve intermediate representations. This enables Any-Time inference (right), allowing the model to exit early and predictXk+1X_{k+1}from any intermediate block via a shared MLM head. Note thatXkX_{k}represents the input forkt​hk^{th}sampling iteration and output images in ELT Any-Time Inference (right) assumek=Kk=K(last sampling step). ReferSection˜2for details of annotations.

Figure 4:Pareto front of FID vs. Inference GFLOPs. The black curve denotes the fit (FID =1922.5⋅G−0.95+1.481922.5\cdot G^{-0.95}+1.48) over pareto-optimal configurations, representing the best achievable FID for a given computational budget. Points are labeled asN​layers×L​loopsN\text{ layers}\times L\text{ loops}. Results demonstrate that ELT scales as effectively as the baseline while remaining significantly more parameter-efficient. Scaling both the model dimension (dd) and the number of loops (LL) follows a predictable efficiency frontier, where larger models with fewer loops often compete with smaller models with more loops at specific target GFLOPs. Trends across faded points show scaling along number of loopsLLin inference, from a single run trained with a certainLm​a​xL_{max}loops.

Figure 5:Scaling with Parameters. We show the best-achievable FID as a function of model parameters (log scale). Each series corresponds to iso-inference-compute configuration for a specific model width (dd). Points are labeled asN​layers×L​loopsN\ \text{layers}\times L\ \text{loops}. Results show that increasing parameter count via model width yields superior FID. Proposed ELT mechanism shows that even parameter-constrained models can achieve similar performance through recursive depth. Each ELT point in the figure is best inference configuration (N×LN\times L) chosen from its corresponding training run (N×LmaxN\times L_{\text{max}}).
Figure 6:Faster Training Convergence with ELT. While using2×2\timesless parameters, ELT improves the training efficiency in diffusion framework.

Table 4:Video Generation on UCF-101.Methods ingrayare pretrained on additional large video data.✓\checkmarkdenotes class-conditional.∗indicates custom resolutions.Δdenotes values from prior publications. No guidance is used.
Figure 7:Impact of ILSD on class-conditional UCF-101 generation.ILSD significantly improves performance for all values ofLLin inference especially whenL≠Lm​a​xL\neq L_{max}.

Figure 9:Class-conditional Image Generation on ImageNet𝟐𝟓𝟔×𝟐𝟓𝟔\bm{256\times 256}. Comparing our proposed Elastic Looped Transformers (left) against vanilla looped transformers (right), which simply repeat transformer layers during a forward pass of diffusion. As shown, standard looping only produces a coherent image when the inference loop count exactly matches its training setup (L=8L=8), with quality severely degrading at all other values. In contrast, our proposed ELT enhances vanilla looping with Self Distillation, maintaining a high-fidelity generation across several evaluated compute budgets.

Comparing our proposed Elastic Looped Transformers (left) against vanilla looped transformers (right), which simply repeat transformer layers during a forward pass of diffusion. As shown, standard looping only produces a coherent image when the inference loop count exactly matches its training setup (L=8L=8), with quality severely degrading at all other values. In contrast, our proposed ELT enhances vanilla looping with Self Distillation, maintaining a high-fidelity generation across several evaluated compute budgets.
